info.title: Antiphony Core API
info.description:
Open-source REST surface for Antiphony — actor identity, canonical audio posts, audio storage, and the auth surface that links them.

## Authentication

Every authenticated endpoint accepts a bearer token via the `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header. Two token types are accepted interchangeably:

- **Firebase ID token** — issued by the Firebase Auth client SDK. Short-lived (~1 hour); refresh client-side.
- **Firebase session cookie** — issued by the hosted dashboard via `POST /api/v1/auth/session` (apps/web only; per-origin Set-Cookie semantics). Longer-lived; useful for server-rendered clients.

Both verify against the same Firebase project. Public endpoints accept missing/invalid tokens but project to the public view shape.

## Envelope

Every JSON response wraps its payload: `{ success: true, data: T }` on success, `{ success: false, error: { message, code?, issues? }, requestId }` on failure. Pagination cursors live inside `data.nextCursor`.

tags.name: Users
tags.description: Actor identity primitives, the viewer's own profile, and public identity projections (profiles, handle resolution).

tags.name: Posts
tags.description: Antiphony canonical audio posts (`dev.antiphony.audio.post`) — create, read, list, and threaded replies with hydrated audio + lifted transcript.

tags.name: Audio
tags.description: Audio storage primitives — the signed-URL proxy and the authenticated / anonymous upload endpoints.

get.summary: List the authenticated viewer's audio posts
get.description: Paginated list of audio posts authored by the viewer within this deploy's origin app. Optional `kind` filter (`prompt`|`reply`). Cursor-paginated by post id.

schema.description: Pagination cursor — the last post id from the prior page

200.description: Paginated list of the viewer's posts

post.description: Creates a `dev.antiphony.audio.post` authored by the viewer. `reply` presence makes it a reply (no title); absence makes it a prompt. The audio is uploaded first (signed-URL flow) and referenced as the `embed`. `originAppId`/`authorId`/`kind` are stamped server-side. Supports `Idempotency-Key`.

schema.description: Optional idempotency key — repeated requests with the same key return the cached response.

409.description: Idempotency conflict — prior request still in flight

get.summary: Get an audio post by id
get.description: Returns the hydrated `AudioPostView` (author + signed audio URL + lifted transcript + viewer state). Scoped to the origin app — a post from another origin app reads as 404.

get.summary: List replies to an audio post
get.description: Returns the post's direct replies (posts whose `reply.parent` is this post), in thread order (oldest first). Cursor-paginated. Scoped to the origin app.

schema.description: Pagination cursor — the last reply id from the prior page

get.description: Returns the full `ProfileViewSelf` (PII-included) for the authenticated viewer.

patch.description: Partial-update profile fields (display name, bio, handle, email, rssFeedUrl, etc.). Handle-swap is transactional. Returns `data: null` on success.

get.description: Returns whether `candidate` is available, taken by another user, owned by the viewer, or invalid.

post.summary: Claim or re-affirm the viewer's handle
post.description: Reserves the candidate handle for the authenticated viewer in an atomic transaction. Echoes the claimed handle + app domain on success.
Soft-deletes (deactivates) the account: marks the user doc, releases the handle, and revokes refresh tokens. Does NOT remove prompts, replies, or org memberships.

get.description: Paginated discovery list of users with a public handle. Cursor-paginated by handle.

schema.description: Pagination cursor — the handle of the last user from the prior page

get.description: Returns every claimed handle in the system. Used by the sitemap generator.

get.summary: Get a user profile by handle
get.description: Returns the full `ProfileView` when the authenticated viewer is the profile owner; otherwise returns `ProfileViewBasic` (handle, displayName, avatarUrl, bio).
Public actor-profile read: the basic (PII-free) profile for the resolved user.

schema.description: The user handle (case-insensitive) or raw UID

post.description: Authenticated multipart/form-data upload with a single `file` field (max 25MB; types: m4a, mp4, mpeg, webm, ogg, wav). Stores the bytes content-addressed and returns the blob ref (`{ $type: "blob", ref: { $link: "<cid>" }, mimeType, size }`) to embed on a post.

blob.description: Canonical AT Protocol blob ref for the stored audio — pass verbatim as the post embed's `audio`.

400.description: Missing/oversized file or unsupported audio type

get.summary: Resolve audio to a signed URL
get.description: Validates the requested object path against the served prefixes (`blobs/`, `audio/`, `prompts/`, `replies/`) and, for reply audio, that the parent prompt exists, then 302-redirects to a short-lived signed URL (~1h TTL, cached ~50m). Anonymous — public audio playback for embeds and public pages.

schema.description: REQUIRED. The canonical storage URL (or object path) of the audio to proxy. Must resolve to an object under `blobs/`, `audio/`, `prompts/`, or `replies/`. Returns 400 if absent.

302.description: Redirect (Location header) to a time-limited signed URL for the requested object.

403.description: Object path outside the served allowlist

404.description: Parent prompt or backing object not found
